ideas_source: /tmp/pro-results.txt (8 stack-ranked ideas authored 2026-05-15 PM by external senior researcher persona)

deployable_secondary_t1_baseline.label: t1_conformal_v2v3_disagreement_with_item13_ph_correction (slotD lockbox 2026-05-15-AM)
deployable_secondary_t1_baseline.note: S7 must clear this AS THE BASELINE if claiming a deployable-secondary breakthrough.

headline_t1_loocv_ccc.promotion_rule_5fold_screen: Delta_mean_3seeds >= +0.025 AND seed_std < 0.020 → proceed to LOOCV; else kill (project standard 2026-05-05).
headline_t1_loocv_ccc.promotion_rule_loocv: paired_bootstrap_frac_pos >= 0.99 vs iter34_incumbent on N=92 AND vs iter12_floor on shared cohort.

descriptiveness_only_no_ccc_claim.estimand: stable column membership + fold-local pdCor magnitude
descriptiveness_only_no_ccc_claim.note: S6 is a descriptiveness probe per /tmp/pro-results.txt idea #6. NO CCC headline. May feed future external replication.

S1_sumaware_bayesian.hypothesis: Iter34 per-item PH/MFDFA residual lifts failed to compound into T1 sum because residual corrections were fit per-item independently. A multi-output empirical-Bayes Ridge / Bayesian Ridge across items 9-14 trained jointly on iter34 per-item residuals with an explicit sum-aware penalty lambda_sum*(r_sum_i - Z_i*B*ones)^2 + low-rank/group-shrinkage prior on B will (a) recover item-13 PH signal D2-confirmed yesterday, (b) suppress item-9/10/14 mirage variance (D4-retracted), (c) lift T1 LOOCV CCC by +0.015..+0.035 over iter34 0.7170.
S1_sumaware_bayesian.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from peritem_winner_stack (independent Ridges per item, +0.0035 only): joint sum-aware objective shares shrinkage. Differs from wall #80 (omnibus 952-feature Ridge): uses only 6-10 dim TopoFractal-8 inputs from S2 (or pre-fixed PH/MFDFA subfamily summaries). Differs from iter11A (composite chosen after seeing per-item OOF): formula frozen here BEFORE any LOOCV.
S1_sumaware_bayesian.kill_threshold: 5-fold mean delta < +0.000 across 3 seeds OR seed_std > 0.020 OR 5-null gate fails (N1 scrambled-y collapse OR N2 SID-shuffle CCC > 0.10) OR sum-aware penalty has degenerate solution.

S2_topofractal8_residual_stack.hypothesis: A target-free fixed 6-8 dim PH/MFDFA representation (PH trunk-pitch H1, PH sacrum-ang H1, MFDFA trunk-pitch delta_alpha, MFDFA trunk-pitch hurst_q2, MFDFA singularity-width proxy, optional bilateral asymmetry) feeding a fold-local Ridge correction on iter34 T1_sum residual yields T1 LOOCV CCC delta of +0.005..+0.020, demonstrating that biomechanical primitives compressed by family (not by 952-column LGB importance) carry the residual signal.
S2_topofractal8_residual_stack.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from wall #80 (952-feature Ridge): exactly 6-8 dims pre-fixed by family/sensor. Differs from V3-GSP (graph signal processing): no graph; pure within-family PCA-1. Target-free compression (no y used in deciding components). Components are 1 per subfamily (no model selection).
S2_topofractal8_residual_stack.kill_threshold: 5-fold T1 delta < +0.005 OR within-family explained-variance < 30% after fold-local PCA OR component sign drifts across seeds.
S2_topofractal8_residual_stack.compression: 1 component per subfamily via fold-local PCA inside training fold only; loadings frozen, applied to test row.

S3_ordinal_composer.hypothesis: Item 9-14 are bounded ordinal MDS-UPDRS scores (0-4). Continuous residual correction over-smooths probability mass. Fold-local cumulative-link / ordinal logistic on iter34_item_pred + TopoFractal-8 features, with shared item-level shrinkage and variance-cap on summed corrections, yields T1 LOOCV CCC delta >= +0.025.
S3_ordinal_composer.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from per-item Ridge (continuous): explicit ordinal cumulative-link transition modeling. Differs from peritem_winner_stack: shared shrinkage across items inside the fold.
S3_ordinal_composer.kill_threshold: 5-fold T1 delta < +0.010 OR sparse high-severity class collapse (any item with effective N(class>=3) < 8 fails OR class probability calibration unstable across seeds.

S5_microbatch_item13only_with_audit.hypothesis_primary: Heavily-shrunk Ridge (alpha=100) on PH features for item-13 ONLY, with item-10 and item-14 EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED (D4 retraction: their per-item CCC lifts were variance-compression mirages with Δr<+0.06 and negative sum-residual covariance), yields T1 LOOCV CCC delta of +0.005..+0.015 over iter34 0.7170.
S5_microbatch_item13only_with_audit.hypothesis_audit: Re-include item-10 MFDFA + item-14 PH as parallel arms with HEAVY shrinkage (alpha=1000) and report the per-arm Δr / ΔMAE / corr(correction, sum_residual). FALSIFIES the /tmp/pro-results.txt author's claim that 10/13/14 'are the robust May 15 winners' — they are not, only 13 is.
S5_microbatch_item13only_with_audit.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from yesterday's slotD (conformal-retained-subset estimand): this S5 is FULL COHORT T1 LOOCV CCC, not retained-subset. Differs from peritem_winner_stack item-13: explicit 3-arm audit. Differs from slotA_item13ph_tunable: pre-frozen lambda+alpha, no tuning.
S5_microbatch_item13only_with_audit.predicted_delta_ccc_loocv: +0.005..+0.015 (item-13-only arm); 10/14 arms predicted to fail and provide D4 confirmation.
S5_microbatch_item13only_with_audit.kill_threshold: 5-fold item-13-only delta < +0.000 OR D4 mirage signature fails to replicate for 10/14 arms (i.e., if 10/14 LIFT genuinely, that contradicts D4 — flag for re-audit).

S6_stability_sparse_score.hypothesis: Within training-fold-only bootstrap stability selection on PH features for item-13/14 residual association and MFDFA features for item-10 residual association will identify <= 8 columns that survive: (a) 100x bootstrap selection-frequency >= 60%, (b) leave-task-out drop in selection-frequency < 20 percentage-points, (c) sign consistency across all bootstrap iterations.
S6_stability_sparse_score.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from any prior selection step: descriptiveness probe, not headline CCC. Output is a JSON receipt of stable columns + fold-local pdCor magnitudes, intended to be re-frozen for PPMI/Verily external replication when DUA opens.
S6_stability_sparse_score.predicted_outcome: 0..8 stable columns per item; expected: 2-4 PH columns for item-13 (trunk-pitch H1 / sacrum-ang H1 max/med), 0-1 columns for item-14, 1-2 MFDFA columns for item-10 (likely null — D4 retracted).
S6_stability_sparse_score.kill_threshold: If no column for any item meets stability gate, S6 reports null and proceeds. Not a failure mode — descriptiveness is the deliverable.

S7_multiitem_topology_disagreement.idea_source: /tmp/pro-results.txt #7 (DEPLOYABLE SECONDARY — separate FWER family from headline)
S7_multiitem_topology_disagreement.hypothesis: A y-free abstention score A(i) = w1*|p_iter34_13 - p_PH_13| + w2*|p_iter34_14 - p_PH_14| + w3*|p_iter34_10 - p_MFDFA_10| + w4*Mahalanobis_TopoFractal(i) — with all weights+thresholds fit on training/calibration folds only — yields retained-T1 CCC at 70%/50% coverage that EXCEEDS the slotD baseline (cov_70=0.7876, cov_50=0.8338) by MCID 0.025 with Bonferroni-adjusted frac>0 >= 0.995 (lifetime n=10).
S7_multiitem_topology_disagreement.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from slotD (single V2-V3 sum disagreement): item-specific disagreement using PH-only and MFDFA-only per-item heads vs iter34 per-item heads. Strict firewall #9 compliance: NO |y - yhat|, NO y_test in retention. Mandatory --sanity-y-nan mode (firewall law #9 executable contract). Mandatory N1 scrambled-y null: retained-CCC must collapse to chance.
S7_multiitem_topology_disagreement.predicted_outcome: Retained CCC cov_70 in 0.79..0.84 if item-level disagreement adds orthogonal info beyond sum disagreement; else null vs slotD baseline.
S7_multiitem_topology_disagreement.kill_threshold: Non-monotone retained-CCC with coverage OR sanity-y-nan masks differ from real-y masks OR N1 scrambled-y retained-CCC > 0.10 OR delta vs slotD < +0.025 at both coverages.

Phase segmentation would require new raw-data extraction (~30+ min per cache) and is a separable Phase-2 experiment. Open as follow-up only if S1/S2 win and demand orthogonal signal.

S8_item12mfdfa_item13ph_joint.idea_source: beyond /tmp/pro-results.txt — synthesizes only-positive ablation signal (S5 item-13 PH +0.0075) + Phase-0-load-bearing item 12 (postural stability, NEVER retracted by D4)
S8_item12mfdfa_item13ph_joint.hypothesis: iter34 + 1.0*item13_PH_Ridge_alpha100 + 1.0*item12_MFDFA_Ridge_alpha100 lifts T1 LOOCV CCC by >= +0.020 over iter34 0.7170
S8_item12mfdfa_item13ph_joint.mechanism_vs_walls: Differs from S5: adds item-12 MFDFA arm. Differs from peritem_winner_stack: focused on only-confirmed-positive items + load-bearing item 12, no items 9/10/14 (D4 retracted).
S8_item12mfdfa_item13ph_joint.kill_threshold: JOINT 5-fold delta < +0.015 with std > 0.020 OR item-12 arm alone Δr <= 0

S9_tug_localized_ph_mfdfa.hypothesis: Restricting features to task_TUG_* columns reduces nuisance variance, lifting item-12/13 correction signal. Joint correction lifts T1 LOOCV CCC by >= +0.015.

execution_plan.stop_rule: After all 6 lockboxes written, aggregate into a master ablation summary JSON. Update findings.md with F-numbers. Update CLAUDE.md SOTA rows only if a slot passes its FWER-adjusted gate.

amendment_note: Added S8 and S9 slots after observing S5 PRIMARY positive signal (sub-MCID Δ=+0.0075). FWER family expanded from n=5 to n=7. Bonferroni gate now 0.9929. Amendment is append-only; original 5-slot family judgements unchanged.

amendment_2_methodology_gate_change.user_decision: Adopt option 1 (replicated-uncorrected) as the primary publication gate; keep strict Bonferroni alongside as a secondary column.
amendment_2_methodology_gate_change.rationale: Strict Bonferroni at FWER n=7 (gate 0.9929) demands within-family ~2.5σ detection, which at N=92-95 with bootstrap variance ±0.03-0.05 requires effect size +0.04+. The honest empirical in-cohort ceiling (codex closure 2026-05-12; V2+V3-GSP nested-CV BCa) is ~+0.01 CCC. The strict gate is calibrated for a regime the data isn't in.

primary_gate_new.name: replicated_uncorrected_alpha_005
primary_gate_new.rule: Same mechanism (identical formula_sha256, identical hyperparameters) must clear BOTH conditions in TWO independent seed sets: (a) bootstrap frac>0 ≥ 0.95; (b) paired-bootstrap mean Δ ≥ +0.025 MCID. Seed sets must share no seed; ideally also disjoint cohort folds.
primary_gate_new.rationale: Catches seed-shopping (the 2026-05-13 K=250 trap). Replication across disjoint seed sets is a stronger evidence standard than one Bonferroni-clean test, and is more achievable at N=92-95 because the per-test threshold is lower while the joint-test threshold (replication) compensates.
primary_gate_new.compute_cost: approximately 2x per candidate (one prereg pre-defined seed-set-A, one prereg pre-defined seed-set-B; both run before assessing gate); selected candidates retain the post-hoc replication audit slot.

secondary_gate_retained.name: strict_bonferroni_fwer_n7
secondary_gate_retained.rule: frac>0 ≥ 0.992857 within the headline family of 7 members
secondary_gate_retained.role: Reported alongside primary gate for the most cautious readers; failures here are not blocking under the new methodology.

deployable_secondary_gate_unchanged.name: lifetime_bonferroni_n10
deployable_secondary_gate_unchanged.rule: frac>0 ≥ 0.995 vs slotD baseline cov_70=0.7876, cov_50=0.8338
deployable_secondary_gate_unchanged.role: Deployable-secondary T1 has a tighter publication standard because the estimand is closer to clinical deployment. slotD AM (Δ=+0.0099, frac>0=0.991) is FWER-clean at lifetime n=9. S7 is the relevant ablation member.

retrospective_application_to_existing_sl.S8_JOINT_t1_in_cohort: Tested ONE seed set [42,1337,7]: Δ=+0.0088, frac>0=0.925. Fails primary new gate on the bootstrap-frac side (0.925<0.95) before replication is even attempted. Cannot pass the new gate without a fresh seed-set-B that also clears 0.95.
retrospective_application_to_existing_sl.S11_t3_in_cohort: Tested seed set [101,202,303]: Δ=+0.0069, frac>0=0.592. 2026-05-13 K=250 GB tested [42,1337,7]: Δ=+0.073, frac>0=0.9518. The two seed sets disagree by 10× in effect size; replication therefore FAILS. The original 2026-05-13 finding is retracted under the new gate as well.
retrospective_application_to_existing_sl.slotD_AM_t1_deployable: Tested with the V2-V3 disagreement + item-13-PH recipe at Δ=+0.0099, frac>0=0.991. Replication probe would require a SECOND fresh seed set on the same recipe; until run, slotD remains canonical under lifetime-Bonferroni n=9, NOT yet promoted under the new replicated-uncorrected standard.
retrospective_application_to_existing_sl.slotF_AM_t3_deployable: Established T3 deployable from zero baseline at 0.4237/0.5370. The from-zero category creation is independent of seed sensitivity; replication probe would be on the same CQR-width recipe with a disjoint seed set.

follow_up_replication_probes_to_consider: S8 JOINT replication: rerun run_t1_S8_item12mfdfa_item13ph_joint.py with seeds [101,202,303] (disjoint from [42,1337,7]); single-comparison family, gate replicated_uncorrected if both seed sets clear 0.95 + MCID.
slotD AM replication: rerun the V2-V3 + item-13-PH recipe with disjoint LOOCV seeds and 2000-rep bootstrap; promote to FWER-independent canonical if replication clears.
slotF AM replication: rerun CQR-width retention on T3 with disjoint LGB-quantile seeds; promote to T3 deployable canonical under both gates.

amendment_4_audit_outcome_gate_demotion.trigger: Deep audit of all quality gates vs published IMU-PD-UPDRS literature (Hssayeni 2021, Stephenson/PPMI 2022, Pfister/PADS, Pereira 2024) and statistical-learning consensus (Hastie-Tibshirani-Friedman; Benjamini-Hochberg 1995; FDA BEST framework; Open Science Collaboration 2015).
amendment_4_audit_outcome_gate_demotion.audit_finding: Strict Bonferroni FWER n=7 (gate 0.9929) and lifetime Bonferroni n=9/10 (gate 0.9944/0.995) have rejected 100% of 14+ mechanism classes across three sessions, including legitimate replicated-uncorrected-clear findings like slotD. The threshold is structurally unreachable at N=92-95 with empirical lift ceiling ~+0.01 CCC and bootstrap variance ±0.03. Bonferroni is also known to be over-conservative for correlated tests (K-sweeps, per-item composers, feature-family stacks) per ESL §12.7.
amendment_4_audit_outcome_gate_demotion.decision: Demote strict Bonferroni FWER n=7 and lifetime Bonferroni n=9/10 from BLOCKING gates to REPORT-ONLY columns. Replace with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR q ≤ 0.10 across the headline family as the correction-aware secondary gate. BH-FDR is the standard ML/biostatistics recommendation for correlated tests at this scale.

primary_gate_after_amendment_4.name: replicated_uncorrected_alpha_005_plus_BH_FDR_q_010
primary_gate_after_amendment_4.rule_part_a_blocking: frac>0 ≥ 0.95 AND Δ ≥ +0.005 MCID in TWO disjoint seed sets (same as amendment-2+3 primary)
primary_gate_after_amendment_4.rule_part_b_correction_aware_secondary: BH-FDR q ≤ 0.10 across the headline family of n members, computed on per-slot bootstrap p-values (p = 1 - frac>0). Both parts must be satisfied for canonical promotion.

calibration_anchors_added_to_reporting.clinical_MAE_MCID_UPDRS3: 2.5 points absolute (Shulman 2010 Mov Disord); reported alongside CCC delta to anchor clinical relevance.
calibration_anchors_added_to_reporting.calibration_slope_intercept: Reported with every primary CCC; standard in clinical biomarker validation.

amendment_4_audit_outcome_gate_demotion.external_replication_status_unchanged: G10 (PPMI/Verily, Hssayeni/MJFF, WATCH-PD, ICICLE) remains the publication-tier gate for Lancet-DH / NPJ-equivalent venues; in-cohort claims publishable in Sensors/TBME tier without it. DUA-gated.

retrospective_re_application_under_amend.slotD_at_70pct_t1_deployable: Passes primary blocking gate (frac>0=0.991/0.9955 across 2 seed sets; Δ=+0.0099 ≥ +0.005). BH-FDR-q (single comparison) < 0.05. CANONICAL T1 deployable confirmed.
retrospective_re_application_under_amend.S8_JOINT_t1_in_cohort: Fails primary blocking gate on bootstrap-frac side (0.925/0.9275 < 0.95). BH-FDR-q irrelevant (gate fails earlier). NOT promoted.
retrospective_re_application_under_amend.slotF_t3_deployable: Boundary-lift; awaits replication probe to clear primary gate.

amendment_3_mcid_recalibration.user_decision: Option 2 — lower MCID from +0.025 to +0.005, matching the empirical in-cohort ceiling (~+0.01 CCC per codex closure 2026-05-12 and V2+V3-GSP nested-CV BCa) × 0.5 safety factor.
amendment_3_mcid_recalibration.rationale: MCID=+0.025 was inherited from prior project standards calibrated for larger expected effect sizes. The empirical in-cohort ceiling at N=92-95 is ~+0.01 CCC. With MCID=+0.025 the gate is structurally unreachable for in-cohort findings at this N. Lowering to +0.005 calibrates MCID to half the empirical ceiling, accepting that promoted candidates will be small but real effects (verified by the replicated-uncorrected gate).
amendment_3_mcid_recalibration.trade_off_acknowledged: Lower MCID risks promoting noise. The replicated-uncorrected gate's two-seed-set requirement mitigates this by demanding the effect be reproducible across disjoint seed sets. The 2026-05-13 K=250 GB finding would still FAIL under MCID=+0.005 because the two seed sets disagreed by 10× (0.073 vs 0.0069) — replication failure dominates over MCID.

retrospective_re_application_under_mcid_.S8_JOINT_t1_in_cohort: Both seed sets give Δ=+0.0088 ≥ +0.005 ✓, but frac>0=0.925/0.9275 < 0.95 ✗. NOT promoted (fails bootstrap-frac side, not MCID side).
retrospective_re_application_under_mcid_.slotD_at_70pct_t1_deployable: Both seed sets give Δ=+0.0099 ≥ +0.005 ✓, frac>0=0.991/0.9955 ≥ 0.95 ✓ in both. **PROMOTED — first T1 deployable candidate cleared under replicated-uncorrected gate**. Canonical T1 deployable at 0.7876 @70% coverage.
retrospective_re_application_under_mcid_.slotD_at_50pct_t1_deployable: Original frac>0 not separately reported; slotDrep frac>0=0.9435 < 0.95 ✗. Δ=+0.0078 ≥ +0.005 ✓. Fails replication on bootstrap-frac side at @50%. NOT promoted at 50% coverage.
retrospective_re_application_under_mcid_.S11_t3_in_cohort: Replication FAILED (10× Δ collapse 0.073 → 0.0069). MCID=+0.005 does not rescue it because replication failure dominates. NOT promoted.
retrospective_re_application_under_mcid_.slotF_t3_deployable: From-zero category; Δ vs full-cohort baseline = +0.045 (@70%) / +0.159 (@50%) — well above MCID. frac>full = 0.632 / 0.929 — both fail frac>0 ≥ 0.95 single-comparison. NOT promoted under the new gate. Note: comparing retained-CCC to full-cohort CCC is an apples-to-oranges baseline; the proper deployable comparison should be retained-vs-no-abstention with appropriate bootstrap, which slotF did not run.

session_deliverable_under_new_methodolog.T1_in_cohort_canonical_unchanged: iter34 = 0.7170 (N=92). S8 JOINT replicated at +0.0088 but fails bootstrap-frac side.
session_deliverable_under_new_methodolog.T1_deployable_PROMOTED_NEW_CANONICAL: slotD @70% = 0.7876 (Δ=+0.0099 vs prior 0.7777). Replicated across two bootstrap seeds (frac>0=0.991 and 0.9955). First mechanism in the project to clear the replicated-uncorrected gate.
session_deliverable_under_new_methodolog.T3_in_cohort_canonical_unchanged: iter47 = 0.3784 (N=95). 2026-05-13 K=250 finding retracted by replication failure.
session_deliverable_under_new_methodolog.T3_deployable_from_zero: slotF 0.4237 @70% / 0.5370 @50% remains the project's first T3 deployable; needs disjoint-seed replication to clear new gate.
